messages.content: hello
Hello! How can I assist you today?
whats the console command to set a npc in skyrim to be non aggresive and run away in combat?

The following console command can be used to make an NPC non-aggressive and flee during combat:

> setav aggression 0

This command sets the NPC's aggression level to 0, which means they will not initiate combat on their own and will flee if attacked.
give me some quotes from inigo

Sure! Here are some memorable quotes from Inigo, a follower in the game Skyrim:

1. "If you think your enemies will wait patiently for you to finish, you have not fought many battles."

2. "I find that absolute silence is usually a sign that something unpleasant is about to happen."

3. "I dislike killing, but if I see a threat to you or myself, I will not hesitate to strike."

4. "I believe everything happens for a reason. Sometimes the reason is we make bad decisions."

5. "There is no enjoyment in killing. It is my least favorite pastime. But sometimes, it is necessary."

6. "Life is an adventure, my friend. You never know what's coming next."

7. "A small but sharp mind can cut through even the toughest problems."

8. "The true measure of a warrior is in their heart, not in their blade."

9. "I prefer to live in the moment. There's too much to enjoy in life to worry about the future or dwell on the past."

10. "I have learned that kindness and understanding can be powerful weapons against hate and ignorance."
any other commands i can use besides "> setav aggression 0" to make a follower non aggresive?

In addition to the `setav aggression 0` command, you can also use these console commands to make a follower non-aggressive:

1. `setav confidence X` - This console command sets the NPC's confidence level to X, where X is a value between 0 and 4. A confidence level of 0 makes the NPC flee from combat, while a value of 4 makes them charge into battle.

2. `setav morality X` - This console command sets the NPC's morality value to X, where X is a value between 0 and 3. A morality value of 0 means the NPC will attack anyone without provocation, while a value of 3 makes them refuse to attack anyone, even if attacked.

3. `setav aggressionbyhealth X` - This console command adjusts the NPC's aggression level based on their health. A value of 0 means the NPC will be non-aggressive when at full health, while a value of 1 means they will be aggressive at all times.

Note: Use these commands with caution, as they can have unintended consequences or disrupt the intended gameplay experience. It's always a good idea to save your game before using any console commands.
what does "avoid threat chance skyrim" do in combat styles for skyrim?

The "Avoid Threat Chance" combat style modifier in Skyrim affects how likely an NPC is to avoid a detected threat while in combat. 

Normally, when an NPC detects a threat (like the player or an enemy), they will move toward and attack that threat. However, with the "Avoid Threat Chance" modifier set to a value greater than 0, the NPC has a chance to avoid combat and stay hidden if they detect a threat.

Setting the "Avoid Threat Chance" to 0 means that the NPC will always engage in combat when they detect a threat, while setting it to a higher value will make it more likely that they will try to stay out of sight and avoid confrontation. 

It is recommended to use this modifier with caution, as it can alter the intended gameplay experience and may make combat encounters easier or more difficult depending on the chosen value.
does this mean if i set it to 1 my follower wont fight?

No, setting the "Avoid Threat Chance" to 1 does not mean your follower won't fight at all. It just means that they have a chance to avoid combat and stay hidden if they detect a threat. 

This setting is typically used to make NPCs more cautious and stealthy in combat, so they may avoid a fight if they don't have an advantage or if they are too weak. However, they will still fight if necessary or if they are commanded to do so.

It's important to note that the "Avoid Threat Chance" modifier is just one of many factors that determine how an NPC behaves in combat. Other factors include their equipped gear, combat style, combat tactics, and their individual personality traits.
what else should i change in combat style if i dont want my follower to fight at all?

If you want your follower to stop fighting completely, you can change their combat style to "None." This will effectively disable their combat abilities and make them useless in battle, but it does not remove them from your party.

To change your follower's combat style to "None," open up the console and click on your follower to select them. Then, type in the following console command:

> setav CA_doCombat 0

This will set their combat ability to 0, effectively disabling their ability to fight.

Note: Disabling your follower's combat abilities can make the game more challenging, especially if you're exploring dangerous areas. It's always a good idea to have backup support in case of emergencies, so consider carefully before you completely disable your follower's combat abilities.
will this command override custom combat styles and will it work on custom followers like inigo?

The `setav CA_doCombat 0` command will completely disable combat for any follower you apply it to, including custom followers like Inigo. 

This console command should override any custom combat style settings that your follower might have set up. However, some custom followers come with their own unique scripts and code that can interact with the game differently than vanilla followers. 

While the command should work on most custom followers, it's possible that some modded followers might be immune to the `setav CA_doCombat` command, or that it could cause unexpected issues. As with any console command, it's always a good idea to save your game before you try it.
does this make them flee when i enter combat?

No, the `setav CA_doCombat 0` command only disables your follower's ability to attack and defend during combat. It does not make them flee when combat begins.

If you want your follower to flee during combat, you will need to adjust their AI settings separately, such as using the `setav aggression 0` command to make them non-aggressive and more likely to flee when attacked, or tweak their behavior in the CK (Creation Kit) or with a third-party mod.

Note that completely disabling your follower's ability to fight or flee can make them vulnerable in combat situations and may require you to provide more protection and assistance during battles. Consider the potential consequences before applying any of these commands or modifications to your follower.

Based on the provided error log, the crash occurred due to an unhandled native exception at the memory location 0x7FF68A6C9F52 (SkyrimSE.exe+10B9F52) on thread 17564. 

The error log also indicates that the NetScriptFramework was running version 15, while the game library was running Skyrim Special Edition version 18. 

The possible relevant objects listed in the log suggest that various NPC and character assets from multiple mods (e.g. Palaces Castles Enhanced, Inigo, Bijin AIO, etc.) were loaded into the game, and the crash may be related to the actors' movement or pathfinding AI.

It's important to keep track of the installed mods and their compatibility with the game version, as this can affect the stability of the game and lead to unexpected crashes and bugs. It is recommended to try disabling mods or updating them to their latest version to see if the issue remains. Additionally, it's also a good idea to regularly save the game to prevent progress loss in the event of a crash.
